front: Painting: series of 14 works showing a Paris boulevard from above at different times and seasons
Painting: one painting in this series depicts a Mardi Gras parade; painted after a similar series of the Rue St. Lazare
Painting: 1896 cityscapes including Morning, An Overcast Day — smokestack and iron bridge; steam ships leaving harbor
Artist: only painter to exhibit at all eight Impressionist exhibitions; oldest Impressionist
Artist: born in St. Thomas (Danish West Indies)
Artist: teacher/mentor of Cezanne; a younger painter called this artist 'le bon dieu'
Artist: created Social Turpitudes (Turpitudes sociales), a series of 28 anti-Semitic drawings
Painting: four pointillist works displayed separately at the Eighth Impressionist Exhibition, including one depicting apple harvesting
Artist: self-portrait with infected drooping eye, painted while hiding in hotel from anti-Dreyfusard rioters
Artist: circulated political drawings in which an old man watches the word 'anarchy' rise with the sun
Artist: painted two peasants and a hay cart directly onto a wooden palette
Artist: urged Van Gogh to live with Dr. Gachet after release from asylum at Saint-Remy
Artist: Derek Walcott's Tiepolo's Hound draws parallels with this artist's move to Paris; illustrated with Walcott's own paintings
Painting: two women walking up around a bend; painted while living in countryside with a younger painter who called the artist 'le bon dieu'
Artist: son Lucien befriended Walter Sickert and helped found the Camden Town Group
